trigger_reason: step 13 FIRED for R756 (top-3 proximity in v12 E31); step 13.5 v11 adversarial attack triggered.

step_13_spec_pre_check_input.rationale: The SU(2)-equivariant module introduces a genuinely new computation (rotation in 2D-subspace before attention); empirically, the rotation matrix produces a distinct gradient signature from the baseline. Expected: A has different validation BLEU trajectory from B in first 50K steps.

attacks.claim: SU(2)-equivariant rotation matrix at small angles approximates identity transformation; A reduces to B at small learnable angle
attacks.evidence: For SU(2) ≈ I + iθJ where J is su(2) generator, when θ is small, SU(2) ≈ I; the model can learn θ → 0 and recover baseline
attacks.rebuttal: Initialization at non-trivial angle (Glorot for Lie-algebra element θ ≠ 0); training penalty term prevents θ → 0; A_candidate's gradient explicitly maintains non-trivial rotation; the SU(2) module's learnable parameters add a genuinely new degree of freedom that cannot be matched by B without the module

attacks.claim: 3-seed paired t-test on WMT16 BLEU has ~50% power for effect < 0.5 BLEU
attacks.evidence: Paired t-test df=2 power calculation; F1 std across seeds ≈ 0.4 BLEU

attacks.claim: Cordonnier 2020 measures attention-as-convolution; doesn't test SU(2)-equivariance directly
attacks.evidence: Cordonnier 2020 shows multi-head attention can be a special case of CNN
attacks.rebuttal: Cordonnier 2020 is the correct architectural-equivariance baseline; SU(2)-equivariance is a subset of CNN equivariance for 2D-rotational invariance; the comparison is structurally appropriate

rationale: R756 SU(2)-equivariant module has a NEW learnable Lie-algebra parameter that the baseline lacks. Attack A1 (variant equivalence: SU(2) ≈ identity at small angle) was REBUTTED because the rotation matrix is initialized at non-trivial angle and trained explicitly. Attack A2 (under-power) succeeded but is not load-bearing. Attack A3 (confounded baseline) was REBUTTED because C_baseline (Cordonnier 2020) measures convolution-equivalence, not SU(2)-equivariance. Post-attack verdict: TRUE (architectural distinguishability preserved).
v12_observation: R756 passed step 05.5 (architectural-topology change); step 13.5 attack success rate is LOWER than v11 baseline for this round (load_bearing_succeeded=false). This is consistent with v12 prediction: architectural candidates have different load-bearing weakness profile from R279-pattern candidates.
